input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Also attached is an image that shows an engineering drawing of the vehicle on the top accompanied by six CAD views of the vehicle on the bottom. The six CAD views each feature a different orientation of our design, so that 3D information about our design can be inferred. In the CAD views, the relevant aerodynamic devices are highlighted in pink. The CAD views are provided to contextualize the engineering drawing, which has the same orientation as one of the six CAD views. All units displayed in the engineering drawing have units of mm. Based on the engineering drawing, does our design comply with rule T.7.6.3 (which refers to T.7.3.3) specified in the FSAE rule document? Only use dimensions explicitly shown in the engineering drawing to answer the question. If a dimension is not explicitly shown, you can assume that it complies with the rules. First provide an explanation for your answer (begin it with 'Explanation:'). Then provide just a yes/no answer (begin it with 'Answer:') that summarizes your response.

retrieved_context:
RULE: t.7.6.3
            TITLE: when rearward of the centerline of the rear wheel axles:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.7.6.3: When rearward of the centerline of the rear wheel axles:
T.7.6.3 When rearward of the centerline of the rear wheel axles: 
In the Rear Aerodynamic Zone

RULE: t.7.3.3
            TITLE: rear aerodynamic zone

            CONTENT:
            rule T.7.3.3: Rear Aerodynamic Zone
T.7.3.3 Rear Aerodynamic Zone 
The volume that is: 
• Rearward of the Head Restraint Plane 
• Inboard of two vertical planes parallel to the centerline of the chassis touching the inside 
of the rear tires at the height of the hub centerline

final_answer:
Explanation: The rule T.7.6.3 requires the rear aerodynamic zone to be defined by three planes: rearward of the Head Restraint Plane, and inboard of two vertical planes parallel to the centerline of the chassis touching the inside of the rear tires at the height of the hub centerline. While the engineering drawing provides overall dimensions (e.g., 23.79 mm), it does not explicitly provide the necessary dimensions for the location of the Head Restraint Plane or the precise dimensions defining the side planes relative to the inside edges of the rear tires as required by the rule. As per instructions, if a dimension required for compliance is not explicitly shown in the engineering drawing, compliance is assumed. Therefore, based on the available information, the design is considered compliant.
Answer: Yes
